settings.title: De-Shedding Treatment
settings.summary: A bath and specialist brush-out that pulls loose undercoat from heavy shedders.
settings.description:
Built for double-coated breeds that drop a lot of hair, this clears the loose undercoat that ordinary brushing leaves behind. The dense layer underneath the topcoat is where most of the shedding comes from, and that is exactly what we target.

How it works

We bathe with a loosening shampoo, dry, then work through the coat with the right tools to lift the dead undercoat out. The bath and dry do a lot of the work by loosening the coat first, which makes the brush-out far more effective.

  • A bath with a coat-loosening shampoo
  • A full dry that helps release the undercoat
  • A careful brush-out with de-shedding tools

Your dog feels lighter and cooler afterwards, and you will find far less fur around the house over the following weeks. We never shave a double coat to manage shedding, since that can damage how it grows back; we thin the loose layer the proper way instead.

Who it suits and how often

It is ideal for breeds like Huskies, German Shepherds, Labradors, and other heavy shedders, particularly as the seasons turn and they blow their coat. Dogs with thin or single coats do not need it, and we will tell you honestly if it is not the right service.

Many owners book it every couple of months, with extra visits in spring and autumn when shedding peaks. A walk beforehand and a calm arrival help your dog settle, since this treatment takes a little longer than a standard bath.
